refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness for operational use.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR specifies a single functional obligation: timely updating of the worksite model according to customer requirements. It does not combine multiple functions or include implementation details, thus it is atomic and solution-neutral.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the latency of worksite model updates under supported scenarios and verify that the 95th percentile latency (p95) meets or is below the customer-defined target (e.g., ≤ 1.0 second).

candidate_dps: Incremental model update mechanism triggered by detected changes
Periodic full model refresh at fixed intervals
Event-driven update pipeline with prioritized processing
Hybrid approach combining incremental updates and periodic full refreshes
selected_dp.selected_dp: Incremental model update mechanism triggered by detected changes

potential_sources: Tight coupling between update mechanism and data acquisition timing
Dependency on external event detection accuracy
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP supports independence by focusing on incremental updates triggered by changes, minimizing unnecessary processing and allowing modular handling of update triggers and model processing.

assumptions_risks: Assumes reliable and timely detection of changes to trigger updates
Risk that incremental updates may miss some changes leading to model inconsistency
Assumes update processing can consistently meet the 1.0 s latency target under peak load
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly defining the update triggering mechanism and its interface with the data acquisition system to ensure modularity and support independent verification of update latency.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the core system logic responsible for managing model updates and integration with other system components.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives data updates and sends status reports related to model changes.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Handles communication for triggering and propagating incremental model updates.

module_containment_scores.notes: The incremental model update mechanism is a core function of the NGHE Solution, with minimal diffusion outside this module.
Vehicle module is more focused on physical control and state, less suitable for managing model updates, leading to high diffusion.
Teleoperation involves remote control and may use model data but is not responsible for update mechanisms, causing moderate diffusion.
Safety Services focus on alerts and safety monitoring, not on model update logic, resulting in poor containment and high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for the incremental model update mechanism, as it centralizes update logic and minimizes diffusion across other modules.
containment_risks: Potential coupling with Telemetry and Network Link interfaces may require careful interface design to avoid tight coupling.
If the update mechanism requires real-time vehicle state data, some diffusion to Vehicle module may occur.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Incremental model update mechanism triggered by detected changes' directly addresses the FR of updating the worksite model within the required timeliness by focusing on incremental updates triggered by changes, which supports meeting the latency acceptance criteria.

diffusion_risks: Potential coupling with Telemetry and Network Link interfaces may require careful interface design to avoid tight coupling.
If the update mechanism requires real-time vehicle state data, some diffusion to Vehicle module may occur.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module has high containment (score 8) and low diffusion (score 2) for the incremental model update mechanism, indicating good architectural packaging. Other modules show higher diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the primary IM boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the FR through the IM is feasible by measuring update latency (p95 ≤ 1.0 s) and ensuring the NGHE Solution module correctly interfaces with Telemetry and Network Link to receive data and propagate updates.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The ownership candidate 'NGHE Solution' clearly encapsulates the DP, minimizing diffusion and providing a clear module boundary for implementation and verification.

recommended_revisions: Explicitly document interface contracts between NGHE Solution and Telemetry/Network Link to manage coupling risks.
Monitor and limit any diffusion of update logic into Vehicle and Teleoperation modules to maintain containment.
Include performance testing scenarios to verify update latency within the NGHE Solution module under realistic operational conditions.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with clear justifications and acceptable architectural packaging. The incremental update mechanism DP directly addresses the FR with latency requirements met, and the NGHE Solution module provides a high containment IM boundary with manageable diffusion risks. Verification feasibility and allocation clarity are confirmed. Recommended revisions focus on documentation and monitoring rather than fundamental decomposition changes, indicating readiness to stop decomposition at this level.

revise.why: To explicitly document interface contracts between NGHE Solution and Telemetry/Network Link modules to manage coupling risks and to monitor diffusion into Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.

assumptions: The incremental update mechanism sufficiently meets the latency and timeliness FR.
NGHE Solution module can encapsulate the DP with minimal diffusion.
Interface contracts can be clearly defined and maintained.
open_risks: Potential coupling with Telemetry and Network Link interfaces may cause tight coupling if not managed.
Possible diffusion of update logic into Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.
Performance under realistic operational conditions needs verification.
evidence_needed: Interface contract documentation between NGHE Solution and Telemetry/Network Link.
Performance testing results demonstrating update latency p95 ≤ 1.0 s.
Monitoring reports on diffusion into other modules.
